title: Ficus coybana
taxonomy: Ficus coybana is classified in the family Moraceae. The genus Ficus is a group of plants commonly known as figs. Ficus coybana is a species within this genus, characterized by its unique characteristics and growth habits. The family Moraceae is a large and diverse group of plants that also includes the genera Artocarpus and Maclura. Ficus coybana is a tropical plant that is native to certain regions.
uses: Ficus coybana, also known as the forest fig, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and fruits have been utilized in treatments for fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. side_effects: Ficus coybana, a plant native to South America, has been associated with some potential side effects due to its sap and latex, which can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and dermatitis in some individuals.
